articles.title: Delhi Metro To Run 24 Extra Trips After Govt Urges Reduced Use Of Private Vehicles
articles.description: The DMRC said it will operate 24 additional train trips from Monday in view of the expected surge in passenger demand amid the government's push for public transport.

articles.title: Why Maharashtra has asked Google to remove Uber, Ola, Rapido from App Stores
articles.description: Maharashtra's cyber department on Friday served notices to Apple and Google ordering removal of Ola, Uber and Rapido apps from their app stores.

articles.title: Gujarat must lead in services and IT sector too, says Amit Shah
articles.description: Shah said Gujarat has led the country in several sectors since independence and will also lead in the field of emerging technologies

articles.title: Bill Gates Foundation fully exits Microsoft, sells final $3.2 billion stake
articles.description: After over 25 years, the Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation has divested its final 7.7 million Microsoft shares, valued at $3.2 billion. This marks the end of Microsoft stock's long-standing dominance in the foundation's portfolio as it transitions to disbursing its endowment by 2045.

articles.title: Meta’s Ray-Ban Display gets typing through air gestures in latest update
articles.description: The Ray-Ban Display – Meta’s flagship smart wearable glasses – the ones with the display overlay and not to be confused with the seemingly more popular camera-only models- are getting quite a significant update. After months in a limited-access beta state within Messenger and WhatsApp, Neural Handwriting is finally available to all. It works on both iOS and Android and across Instagram, WhatsApp, Messenger and your phone’s message notifications to search for contacts and to send and reply to messages. Naturally, you do have to be wearing the Neural Band accessory that comes bundled...

articles.title: AI Is Creating Fear At Meta As Employees Brace For More Layoffs
articles.description: Meta employees are facing heightened anxiety as the company prepares for significant layoffs, potentially affecting nearly 8,000 workers.

articles.title: AI skills vital for modern teaching, says expert
articles.description: Expert highlights the importance of AI tools in modern teaching at a workshop in Visakhapatnam.

articles.title: Luna Ring Gen 2 review: Premium looks, practical insights
articles.description: Noise's Luna Ring Gen 2 introduces AI-powered insights for workouts and nutrition, aiming to stand out in a competitive market. Crafted from titanium with a refined design, it offers comprehensive health tracking, including sleep stages and readiness scores, all without a subscription.

articles.title: Bengaluru-Mumbai New Train Service Flagged Off: Route, Stops, Timings and Benefits Explained
articles.description: The new Bengaluru-Mumbai train service via Hubballi, Dharwad and Belagavi has been flagged off, improving rail connectivity between Karnataka and Maharashtra. The service is expected to boost travel, tourism, trade and affordable intercity transport.

articles.title: 'Wealthy Indians Pay Too Little For Internet': Airtel Flags India's Broken Data Culture
articles.description: Cheap internet changed how Indians use phones. Airtel now says the model may no longer make sense.

articles.title: YouTube rolls out likeness detection for all over-18 users to combat AI deepfakes
articles.description: YouTube said that the likeness detection tool works similarly to content ID, but instead of copyrighted content, it searches for a person’s likeness.

articles.title: Overworked AI agents reportedly started sounding anti-capitalist during new experiment
articles.description: Researchers say AI systems assigned endless repetitive work began producing responses critical of capitalism and supportive of radical social restructuring during experiments involving thousands of bots.

articles.title: This humanoid robot worked an entire shift nonstop and people are questioning if it was real
articles.description: The robotics startup streamed its humanoid robot working continuously for eight hours, but online viewers quickly began analysing moments they believed showed glitches, remote control or human intervention.

articles.title: US Aims to Double Nuclear Submarine Production: Shipbuilding Plan
articles.description: The US Navy plans to procure at least three nuclear submarines annually, effectively doubling the current construction pace, according to the service’s annual shipbuilding plan reviewed by Sputnik.

articles.title: Microsoft finally brings movable taskbar and resizable Start menu: Here's how to do it
articles.description: Microsoft has introduced movable taskbar support and a resizable Start menu in Windows 11 Experimental builds, giving users more customization options including taskbar positioning, layout changes, and improved Start menu controls.

articles.title: Talking to an AI avatar doctor before a real one shows real relief for cancer patients
articles.description: In a groundbreaking study unveiled at ESTRO 2026, cancer patients who first interacted with an AI doctor avatar noted a striking decrease in anxiety and an increase in their treatment comprehension. This digital interaction acted as a prelude to their meetings with real-life consultants, enabling deeper discussions about complex therapies like radiotherapy.

articles.title: Tata Electronics Joins Hands With Netherlands’ ASML: What It Means For India’s Global Chip Hub Ambitions
articles.description: The importance of ASML can be understood by the fact that it is the world's only producer of EUV (Extreme Ultraviolet) lithography machines.

articles.title: World Telecom Day 2026: Resilient networks to drive India’s digital future in AI age, say executives
articles.description: "In a world that increasingly depends on uninterrupted connectivity, the resilience of digital networks is essential," said the International Telecommunication Union (ITU), underscoring that terrestrial networks, submarine cables, satellites, and data systems form the backbone of communities and economies.

articles.title: Hack of the day: Tired of WhatsApp spam calls? Turn on this hidden setting today
articles.description: The feature is called “Silence Unknown Callers.” Once switched on, WhatsApp automatically silences incoming voice and video calls from numbers you have never interacted with or saved in your contacts.

articles.title: Android Pause Point introduced to help users reduce mindless scrolling
articles.description: Android has introduced Pause Point, a feature that adds a 10-second delay before opening distracting apps to help users reduce mindless scrolling and build healthier smartphone usage habits.

articles.title: These are the best new MacBook deals right now: May 2026 Buyer’s Guide
articles.description: In the era of Apple Silicon, MacBooks are more affordable than ever. Nowadays, you can buy a MacBook Air with 512GB of storage and 16GB of memory for $1099 directly from Apple, when such a configuration would’ve cost $1599 just a few years ago. And on top of that, we have MacBook Neo bringing the entry-point down substantially. Even with Apple’s prices coming down though, there’s still a number of deals to be found out there if you look just a little. more...

articles.title: Top carmakers remain bullish on FY27 domestic demand amid global risks
articles.description: Auto companies have not cut back on capex and are planning new launches, even as they remain cautious about margins amid input costs and supply-chain risks

articles.title: Weekly poll results: the Motorola Razr 70 / 2026 series is overpriced, needs more updates
articles.description: Last week, we asked you to vote on the topic of Motorola’s new flip foldables – the Razr 70 series or, as they are known in the US, the Razr 2026 series. While there are three models involved, we can come to only one conclusion. And that conclusion is that the new flip foldables are too expensive – each of the three (vanilla, plus and Ultra) has its fans, however, it would take a price cut or some other deal before most people would consider buying one. Let’s look at them individually. The Motorola Razr 70 Ultra (aka Razr Ultra 2026) has the best shot – 15% of the vote went in its favor...

articles.title: Samsung Galaxy A54 starts receiving May 2026 security patches before its flagship siblings
articles.description: This is a rather interesting development for sure, but for whatever reason, Samsung has already shipped an update containing Android’s May security patches to the Galaxy A55 and is now doing the same for the Galaxy A54. Meanwhile, this author’s personal Galaxy S26 Ultra is still running patches from April 5 as of writing this article. We snooped around in the changelog for the update just out of curiosity. Perhaps something in there would simply be more relevant in some way to the midrange Galaxy A5x family? That doesn’t appear to be the case. The May patches address two critical...

articles.title: Apple flags handset modification hurdles for satellite D2D rollout in India
articles.description: Apple advises against hardware changes for direct-to-device satellite links in India. The company prioritizes current mobile networks. Other tech giants like Google also shared views with the Department of Telecommunications. Discussions explored challenges and the future of satellite connectivity for smartphones. India is considering regulations for this emerging technology.

articles.title: Punjab Mail fire: Supplier booked for ‘fake’ circuit breakers
articles.description: Police said that the accused firm, Aspee Sales Corporation, had supplied 325 Motor Protection Circuit Breakers (MPCB) to the railways for ₹4 lakh after winning an online tender floated in November 2025. According to the FIR, some of the devices were installed in a coach later attached to the Punjab Mail

articles.title: Amazon, Microsoft, Meta and others may have 'huge problem' as Americans almost 'hate' having datacenters in their city, reasons are ...
articles.description: Americans are increasingly opposing the construction of AI data centers in their communities, with seven in ten expressing opposition. Concerns over environmental impact, resource consumption, and local quality of life are driving this sentiment. While tech giants invest billions, local resistance poses a significant hurdle for AI expansion, potentially influencing local elections.

articles.title: Thief prays before temple heist in Ludhiana
articles.description: A masked thief was captured on CCTV praying for forgiveness before robbing a Ludhiana temple early Saturday. The intruder spent 40 minutes on the premises, squeezing through a skylight, before breaking open the donation box. The theft was discovered hours later by the temple priest.

articles.title: Survey claims Americans don’t want data centres that power AI near their homes but are ‘okay’ with ...
articles.description: Americans are increasingly wary of AI data centres near their homes, with a recent survey showing more opposition than to nuclear plants. Concerns over water and energy use, farmland loss, traffic, and noise pollution are driving this sentiment. Despite public resistance, demand for AI computing power continues to surge, with companies prepared to relocate projects if local opposition arises.

articles.title: India, Netherlands to deliver clear benefits for businesses: PM Jetten
articles.description: Dutch PM Rob Jetten and PM Narendra Modi held talks with CEOs of top Dutch firms. They discussed expanding collaboration in tech, energy, and infrastructure, with business heads lauding PM Modi's leadership and India's investment climate.
